constraints.expression: consumer verifies Ed25519 cluster_signature over RFC 8785 canonical JSON of all-other-fields (including ingested_emission) using cluster_signature signer's public key
constraints.message: Cluster Ed25519 signature MUST verify against the signer derived from signer_cluster_id + signer_key_version per the consumer's keyring resolution policy.
constraints.evaluation_note: Cryptographic signature verification is consumer-side per NF-1 (library/runtime boundary). The library evaluator SKIPS this rule and surfaces it in the UnverifiedObligationsManifest under the 'x-crypto-bearing' path. Consumers MUST: (1) reconstruct RFC 8785 canonical JSON of all fields except `cluster_signature`; (2) resolve the signer's public key from `signer_cluster_id` + `signer_key_version` via their Keyring; (3) verify the Ed25519 signature; (4) reject the record on signature mismatch. The expression carries the narrative obligation rather than a parseable DSL expression because the rule is runtime-deferred.

constraints.message: signer_key_id MUST equal sha256_hex(signer_cluster_id || ':' || signer_key_version). The wire field is a derivation of the cluster identity + key version; a mismatch indicates either a misconfigured key-derivation pipeline on the producer side or wire-payload tampering.
constraints.evaluation_note: FR-B2 schema-side reinforcement of CT-08 cluster-identity binding. The LOCAL builtin `signer_key_id_matches_derivation` (NEW in PR-A3.4) computes the sha256 of `signer_cluster_id || ':' || signer_key_version` and compares against the asserted `signer_key_id`. The derivation is byte-stable cross-runner: the colon delimiter is ASCII 0x3A; both inputs are UTF-8 encoded as-is (no NFC normalization at the derivation layer — consumers MUST author cluster_ids in NFC form upstream). Case-insensitive comparison: uppercase hex on the wire matches lowercase derivation. Library-evaluated; no consumer state required.

constraints.message: Canonical-JSON form of the envelope MUST be ≤ 4096 bytes (NFR-4). Reduce the payload size or split across multiple envelopes.
constraints.evaluation_note: NFR-4 4 KB canonical-JSON cap. The LOCAL builtin `canonical_size_cap` (NEW in PR-A3.4) computes Buffer.byteLength(safeCanonicalize(envelope), 'utf8') and asserts ≤ 4096. The cap value is also declared on the schema's TypeBox metadata as `'x-canonical-size-cap-bytes': 4096` for tooling that reads schema metadata directly. Library-evaluated; no consumer state required.

constraints.message: Nonce MUST be unique per signer within the configured sliding window (FR-C1). A repeat (signer_id, nonce) pair within the window is a replay candidate.
constraints.evaluation_note: FR-C1 replay detection. Library-evaluated when the consumer supplies `nonce_window` state via validate() options.nonce_window; deferred to consumer with `NONCE_CONTEXT_DEFERRED` manifest entry when state absent (per PR-A3.3 deferred-context pattern). The check operates on the inner Tier-1 emission's `agent_id` + `nonce` because nonces are agent-scoped (one agent emitting the same nonce twice within the window is the replay surface).

constraints.message: Per-cluster sequence + key-version monotonicity (FR-C2). Sequence MUST strictly increase per (signer, key_version); key_version MUST NOT regress across the cluster's history.
constraints.evaluation_note: FR-C2 per-cluster sequence monotonicity. Library-evaluated when the consumer supplies `sequence_state` via validate() options.sequence_state; deferred with `SEQUENCE_CONTEXT_DEFERRED` when state absent. CT-08 cluster-id mismatch fires BEFORE state-map lookup. CT-03 string→BigInt parsing uses the numeric-regex pre-validator (no try/catch). The DSL expression composes the 'sequence' field from the inner Tier-1 emission via consumer-side resolution in v8.6.0; future cycles MAY widen the DSL grammar to dotted-path field references like 'ingested_emission.sequence'. For v8.6.0, consumers wanting precise per-cluster sequence enforcement at validate() time MUST hoist the sequence/agent_id fields to the Tier-2 envelope or pre-flatten before validate().

constraints.expression: consumer cross-checks chain via chain_validator_prev_hash with consumer-supplied chain context including this envelope plus all ancestors back to genesis
constraints.message: prev_envelope_hash MUST link this envelope to its predecessor's canonical-JSON sha256 hash; the chain MUST anchor at the documented genesis sentinel (FR-C3).
constraints.evaluation_note: FR-C3 chain validity. Library-evaluated when the consumer supplies `chain_ledger` via validate() options.chain_ledger AND assembles the chain into a single array; in v8.6.0 the typical case is consumer-side evaluation because the chain is multi-record and the validate() call here operates on a single envelope. The DSL builtin `chain_validator_prev_hash` operates on an array argument; runtime-deferred is the right classification for the single-record validate() surface. Consumers iterating a chain history call the standalone evaluator at `src/constraints/builtins/chain-validator-prev-hash.ts` directly. The schema's `'x-chain-bearing': true` flag opts into the FR-A4 fail-closed contract (validate({ failClosed: true }) rejects when chain context absent).
